title: Post-Termination Access Revocation within 24 Hours

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-004-6 R4 Part 4.2.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-004-6 R4 Part 4.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.12: Post-Termination Access Revocation within 24 Hours.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.12' running on index=hr sourcetype=hr:termination, index=wineventlog EventCode=4726, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=hr sourcetype=hr:termination earliest=-14d
| eval term_epoch=strptime(term_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| rename employee_id as user
| join type=left user [
    search index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4726 earliest=-14d
    | stats earliest(_time) as disable_epoch by TargetUserName
    | rename TargetUserName as user
  ]
| eval delta_h=round((disable_epoch-term_epoch)/3600,2)
| where isnull(disable_epoch) OR delta_h>24
| table user, term_time, disable_epoch, delta_h
description: We measure hours from HR termination timestamp to Active Directory account disable for BES personnel, evidencing the one-day removal expectation.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes hr, wineventlog and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security, hr:termination, hr:termination`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Ensure 4726 from all in-scope domains. (2) Normalize time zones to UTC. (3) Page IAM when SLA missed. (4) Optionally join CyberArk disable events as supplemental proof. Indexes required: `index=hr`, `index=wineventlog`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `sourcetype=hr:termination`, `sourcetype=hr:termination``.
visualization: Table (SLA misses), Column chart (delta hours), Single value (miss count). Key fields: `delta_h`, `disable_epoch`, `term_epoch`, `term_time`, `user`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, registered change tickets, and approved work can look like noncompliance in raw telemetry; confirm against the current change record before you escalate.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=hr` `sourcetype=hr:termination`, `index=wineventlog` `EventCode=4726`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure 4726 from all in-scope domains. (2) Normalize time zones to UTC. (3) Page IAM when SLA missed. (4) Optionally join CyberArk disable events as supplemental proof.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hr sourcetype=hr:termination earliest=-14d
| eval term_epoch=strptime(term_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| rename employee_id as user
| join type=left user [
    search index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4726 earliest=-14d
    | stats earliest(_time) as disable_epoch by TargetUserName
    | rename TargetUserName as user
  ]
| eval delta_h=round((disable_epoch-term_epoch)/3600,2)
| where isnull(disable_epoch) OR delta_h>24
| table user, term_time, disable_epoch, delta_h
```

Understanding this SPL

**Post-Termination Access Revocation within 24 Hours (CIP-004-6 R4 Part 4.2)** — We measure hours from HR termination timestamp to Active Directory account disable for BES personnel, evidencing the one-day removal expectation.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=hr` `sourcetype=hr:termination`, `index=wineventlog` `EventCode=4726`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hr; **sourcetype**: hr:termination. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hr, sourcetype=hr:termination, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **term_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **delta_h** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(disable_epoch) OR delta_h>24` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Post-Termination Access Revocation within 24 Hours (CIP-004-6 R4 Part 4.2)** — We measure hours from HR termination timestamp to Active Directory account disable for BES personnel, evidencing the one-day removal expectation.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=hr` `sourcetype=hr:termination`, `index=wineventlog` `EventCode=4726`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: delta_h, disable_epoch, term_epoch, term_time, user.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP firewall log, Windows Security, or the asset register—whichever the search is built to mirror. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (SLA misses), Column chart (delta hours), Single value (miss count)
grandmaExplanation: We measure hours from HR termination timestamp to Active Directory account disable for BES personnel, evidencing the one-day removal expectation. That is important for the big power grid, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Authentication data model (Authentication.Authentication).
